name: Up-Side Apple Cake
recipeIngredient: 2 large Granny Smith apples (peeled, cored, and sliced into 1/4-inch slices)
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 9-inch springform or round cake pan (1 1/2 inches deep).
2. Melt 2 tablespoons butter with brown sugar in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat.
3. Add apple slices to the skillet and cook over medium heat until apples begin to soften, about 4 minutes, stirring often.
4. Arrange the cooled apple slices evenly over the bottom of the prepared cake pan.
5. Cream the remaining 1/2 cup butter with granulated sugar until light.
6. Add Egg and beat until smooth.
7. Mix in the applesauce and blend well.
8. Combine flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt in a small bowl; stir into the creamed mixture.
9. Spread batter evenly over the apple slices.
10. Place the pan on a baking sheet and bake in the middle rack of a 350°F oven until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean (about 40-45 minutes).
11. Let cool for 15 minutes before removing from the pan using a flexible knife.
12. Invert the pan onto a serving platter, replacing any apple slices that may remain in the pan.
13. Serve warm with Cinnamon Dream Topping:
- Beat cream cheese and sour cream until smooth.
- Gradually add cinnamon, then powdered sugar, mixing well after each addition until smooth.
- Let stand 15 minutes at room temperature before serving.
